blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: What made you write it down?

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: In English. When I said what I said about the narrator. You wrote it down.
She'd seen that. I didn't know she saw that.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: It felt true. Like — I'd been thinking something like that but I couldn't have said it that way.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Like he can't picture himself having anything.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. Like he watches everyone else's life from just outside it.
She looked at me differently after that. Not surprised exactly. More like — confirmed. Like she'd been wondering if I heard it and now she knew I had.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Most people in that class just write down the plot.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: The plot isn't the part that sticks.
She said it quiet, like it was something she'd been saying to herself for years without anyone agreeing.
The 34 didn't come. We kept talking.
